headline: 'I almost faint every time I stand up': In cramped student housing, studying becomes impossible during French heatwave
subtitle: Students living in tiny apartments with no way to circulate air are struggling with extreme temperatures. For many of them, this coincides with exam season.

headline: US Supreme Court clears way for Trump administration's anti-immigration policies
subtitle: In a historic reversal of the country's tradition of welcoming newcomers, on Thursday, the justices authorized the administration's move to revoke protections that shielded 350,000 Haitians and 6,000 Syrians from deportation.

headline: 250 years of American independence: Thomas Jefferson and John Adams, 'the North and South poles of the American Revolution'
subtitle: 'America 250' (4/13). Fifty years to the day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, two of its architects died: the enigmatic Thomas Jefferson and the impulsive John Adams. This coincidence only strengthened, among the population, the sense of an unprecedented destiny promised to their nation.

headline: Keith Haring's final work finds a home in Paris's 'artists' church'
subtitle: The parish at Saint-Eustache in Paris is unveiling the restored Saint-Vincent-de-Paul chapel, funded by the World Monuments Fund. The renewed setting gives fresh prominence to 'The Life of Christ,' the American artist's final work before his death in 1990, during the AIDS epidemic.

headline: The unavoidable rise of a less American world
subtitle: The war in the Middle East has sped up a realignment that was already underway, forcing the US's partners to rethink flexible alliances without creating overly strong dependencies.

headline: Serving a husband, God and America: Young female conservatives gather at San Antonio's Turning Point summit
subtitle: In early June, around 3,000 women attended the Women's Leadership Summit in San Antonio, Texas. This annual event brings together a variety of conservative figures and influencers, relying on the extensive network of Turning Point USA, founded by right-wing activist Charlie Kirk, who was murdered in 2025. After convincing a majority of young men to vote for Trump in 2024, the organization now targets female voters from Gen Z.
